title: Thelypteris scalpturoides
intro: Thelypteris scalpturoides, also known as the toothed maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris scalpturoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris scalpturoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Thelypteris scalpturoides is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. This species is further classified within the genus Thelypteris, which is a genus of ferns native to the Americas. The classification of Thelypteris scalpturoides also places it within the order Polypodiales, a large order of ferns. The kingdom and division of this plant are classified as Plantae and Pteridophyta, respectively. Furthermore, the family Thelypteridaceae is often placed within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises vascular plants without flowers or seeds.
uses: Thelypteris scalpturoides is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
